technique.title: Jump Technique
technique.desc: Phases of high jump: approach, takeoff, flight

errors.title: Common Errors
errors.desc: Analysis of frequent mistakes and corrections

warmup.title: Warm-Up
warmup.desc: Warm-up protocols and box breathing timer

peaking.heading: Peaking Rules
rules: No new exercises within 3 weeks of competition
Minimum 2 specific sessions per week in PRE_COMP

phases.name: Approach
phases.desc: 8-10 curved steps. Last 3 steps — increasing speed. Arc angle 30-45° to bar.

phases.name: Penultimate step
phases.desc: Longer last step — center of mass lowers. Foot placed heel-first, roll.

phases.name: Takeoff
phases.desc: Explosive extension at ankle, knee, hip. Swing free leg and arms upward.

phases.name: Flight (Fosbury Flop)
phases.desc: Arch at lower back, clear bar sequentially: back → hips → legs.

phases.name: Landing
phases.desc: On mat, back first. Delay leg extension until hips clear bar.

items.fix: Start arc at step 4-5, gradually increase curve radius
Takeoff point 60-80 cm from bar projection
Synchronized explosive arm drive upward at takeoff
Arch only after shoulders have cleared bar
Extend legs upward after hips clear bar

phases.name: GPP
phases.focus: Base strength, high volume, low intensity

phases.name: SPP
phases.focus: Sport-specific prep, jump work, rising intensity

phases.name: PRE_COMP
phases.focus: Speed sharpening, volume drops, high intensity

phases.name: COMP
phases.focus: Competition peak, minimal volume, max intensity

items.cause: Excessive lordosis in flop, weak core

warmupPage.title: Warm-Up
warmupPage.subtitle: Protocols for training, competition and recovery
warmupPage.breathingSub: Inhale — hold — exhale — hold, 4 seconds each
warmupPage.noTemplates: No protocols yet. Add via Reference → Templates

success_visualization.title: Success Visualization
success_visualization.body: Mentally replay your perfect jump: approach, takeoff, flight. In full detail.
success_visualization.tip: 3 min before sleep: picture 5 perfect jumps

pre_comp_routine.title: Pre-Competition Routine
pre_comp_routine.body: A standard sequence of actions reduces anxiety and sharpens focus.

breathing_focus.title: Breathing Focus
breathing_focus.body: Controlled breathing slows your heart rate and brings you into the zone.
breathing_focus.tip: 4-4-4-4 box breathing: 3 cycles before the start

positive_self_talk.title: Positive Self-Talk
positive_self_talk.body: Replace 'don't mess up' with 'I am ready' — words shape your mental state.
positive_self_talk.tip: Create 2-3 personal mantras and repeat during warm-up

pressure_release.title: Pressure Release
pressure_release.body: Pressure is a privilege. It means you are good enough for this moment.
pressure_release.tip: Ask yourself: 'What's the worst that can happen?' — then accept it

performance_mindset.title: Performance Mindset
performance_mindset.body: Only evaluate what you control: technique, approach, focus.
performance_mindset.tip: Forget the result in the moment — focus on the process

step2.title: 30 min out: Visualization
step2.desc: 5 perfect jumps in your head, find a quiet place

step5.title: Go
step5.desc: Trust your training. You are ready.
